# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Curl;

class PreviewController extends Controller
{
    public function showPreview(Request $request)
    {
        $url = $request->input('url');

        if (filter_var($url, FILTER_VALIDATE_URL) === false) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Invalid URL'], 400);
        }

        $curl = new Curl();
        $response = $curl->get($url);

        if ($curl->hasError()) {
            return response()->json(['error' => 'Failed to fetch page content'], 500);
        }

        // Optionally parse the HTML to extract title and meta description
        $html = $response;
        $doc = new \DOMDocument();
        @$doc->loadHTML($html);

        $title = $doc->getElementsByTagName('title')->item(0)->nodeValue ?? 'No Title';
        $description = $doc->getElementsByTagName('meta');
        $descriptionContent = '';
        foreach ($description as $node) {
            if ($node->getAttribute('name') === 'description' && $node->hasAttribute('content')) {
                $descriptionContent = $node->getAttribute('content');
                break;
            }
        }

        return response()->json([
            'url' => $url,
            'title' => $title,
            'description' => $descriptionContent,
            'content' => substr($html, 0, 200) . '...'
        ]);
    }
}
